basics.name: Efe Varol Bedelcigil
basics.label: Electrical & Electronics Engineering Student
basics.summary: 3rd-year Electrical and Electronics Engineering undergraduate with a strong interest in Control Systems and Robotics for autonomous platforms. Supports this interest with hands-on experience in real-time embedded control and power electronics projects. Targeting an Internship or Candidate Engineering position in the defense industry to contribute to R&D processes and pursue a long-term career.

projects.name: STM32 Based Stabilized Platform (Gimbal)
projects.summary: Designed and implemented a 2-axis real-time robotic stabilization system, developing cascaded PID control loops and IMU-based sensor fusion, deployed on STM32 under deterministic RTOS scheduling (FreeRTOS).
highlights: Developed embedded firmware for PWM actuator control and feedback signal processing.
Performed iterative PID tuning and system-level verification & validation (V&V) to improve platform stability under dynamic disturbances.

projects.name: Autonomous Surface Vehicle (ASV – MAVİ İNCİ)
projects.summary: System Integration role. Designed and implemented safety-critical emergency stop systems (remote wireless & physical kill-switches) to ensure fail-safe operation.
highlights: Contributed to system integration and modelling.
Supported power budget analysis and embedded hardware architecture definition.
Ensured fault-tolerant communication of onboard units under mission-critical constraints.

projects.name: Linear DC Power Supply
projects.summary: Designed, simulated, and validated a 0-8 V/0-1 A CC/CV linear DC power supply.
highlights: Designed low-level ripple filtering and PCB-level thermal considerations to ensure safe and reliable operation.

projects.name: 32-Bit RISC Processor Design
projects.summary: Designed and verified a 32-bit single-cycle RISC processor in VHDL.
highlights: Implemented datapath, control logic, and finite state machines.
Conducted functional test benches and FPGA synthesis for correctness validation.

volunteer.summary: Leading a 500+ member technical organization; coordinating committees and strategic planning.
highlights: Established structured documentation and reporting processes to ensure sustainable knowledge transfer.

volunteer.organization: IEEE Ege - Teach and Make Project
volunteer.summary: Mentored students on robotics, algorithm development and embedded systems with emphasis on safety awareness.
highlights: Designed and instructed a DC-DC Converter Workshop covering Buck/Boost topologies.

volunteer.summary: Organized technical visits to leading companies, strengthening industry-academia networking.
highlights: Mentored a 30+ member team on professional communication and logistics.
